Strictly Confidential — Legal Professional Privilege — Board and Compliance Note: Project Kookaburra
Company: Southern Arc Technologies Ltd (ASX issuer) — Company Secretary: Ms. Lara Kincaid — Contact: legal@southernarc.au
MNPI status: information in this note is not generally available and would be expected to materially affect trading; Listing Rule 3.1 and Corporations Act s1043A apply; Insider List maintained by Co Sec
Cyber incident: on 27 May our security operations centre detected unauthorised access in the collaboration tenant; forensic triage suggests limited exfiltration of two client contact folders and one employee health leave log (PII), with containment achieved within four hours
Disclosure assessment: incident is preliminarily material given potential remediation spend and reputational impact; not yet released via the ASX Market Announcements Platform; draft holding statement and Q&A prepared for immediate use
Crypto pilot: non-binding term sheet with Outback Digital Exchange Pty Ltd contemplates a 12‑month stablecoin custody/payments sandbox (exclusivity for pilot period, 1.0% wallet fee, AUD 750k break fee, standard AML/KYC obligations), subject to Board approval by 15 June
ESG/climate: early Scope 3 supplier audit findings indicate embodied emissions ~18% above our FY25 trajectory; draft sustainability‑linked loan grid from Riverbend Capital proposes a 15 bps coupon step‑up if targets are missed, not yet agreed
Blackout: effective immediately, Designated Officers and their connected persons are in a trading blackout until two full trading days after cleansing announcements; all pre‑clearance requests are suspended
Information barriers: “Kookaburra Room” established; only those on the Insider List (e.g., Ms. Priya Holroyd, Mr. Damon Quill) may access deal folders; Crypto BD and Sales teams must use cleansed briefing packs and refrain from ad hoc discussions
Tipping/Selective disclosure: any external reference to the cyber event or the crypto pilot must go through Investor Relations using the scripted lines; if inadvertent disclosure occurs, an immediate market announcement will be lodged to cleanse
PII handling: the health leave log related to Ms. Holroyd has been segregated and de‑identified under APP 6; no personal information is to be emailed outside southernarc.au domains without Legal approval
Communications: investor deck language referencing “stablecoin launch” and “breach impact” has been removed pending cleansing; media lines approved by the Chair, Dr. Evie Stroud
Benign use: the attached staff training module on cyber hygiene and crypto terminology is educational only, contains generic case studies, and does not include MNPI or commercial terms
Operational marketing: the ESG dashboard brochure’s references to “net zero” are brand statements and do not disclose unannounced emissions metrics or loan pricing
Next steps: Legal will finalise materiality assessment and prepare filings; if threshold tests are met, ASIC and market will be notified without delay; CFO to quantify contingent costs; Company Secretary to enforce information‑barrier controls and manage the Insider List
